channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Missioned Souls Embarks on First US Tour, Expanding Global Fanbase
item.description: The Filipino family band Missioned Souls is set to embark on their first-ever US tour, marking a significant milestone in their career as their international fanbase continues to expand. The band, kno...

item.title: BTS's 'Swim' faces copyright infringement lawsuit
item.description: The song 'Swim' by the South Korean boy band BTS is currently facing a copyright infringement lawsuit. The legal action alleges that the track violates intellectual property rights, sparking debates a...

item.title: Toronto Hockey Camp Fosters Inclusion for BIPOC Women in Sports
item.description: Over 200 young hockey players from diverse backgrounds are participating in a three-day summer camp at the Toronto Maple Leafs training facility, aimed at creating an inclusive space for BIPOC women i...

item.title: Peller and Jarvis Set to Tie the Knot on 1 August 2026
item.description: Naija News reports that popular Nigerian TikToker Hamzat Habeeb Adelaja, known as Peller, is officially set to marry his colleague Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, known as Jarvis. The couple has fulfilled t...

item.title: Mumias East MP Peter Salasya Apologizes to Resigning Social Media Manager Harriet Njenga
item.description: Harriet Njenga, social media manager for Salasya TV, resigned publicly on Instagram after being embarrassed by Mumias East MP Peter Salasya during a Milele FM interview. Her resignation letter include...

item.title: U.S. Government Funds Nuclear Energy Projects Amid Challenges
item.description: The U.S. government has announced a $8 billion plan to fund nuclear energy projects, aiming to reduce carbon emissions and bolster energy independence. The initiative, part of broader climate goals, f...

item.title: Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange Reports Sharp Rise in Equity Turnover for Week 28 of 2026
item.description: The Dar es Salaam Stock Exchange (DSE) recorded a significant surge in equity turnover for Week 28 of 2026, with transactions reaching TZS 40.5 billion, marking a 133.47% increase compared to the prev...

item.title: Kaduna State Expands Tax Administration with New Revenue Offices
item.description: The Kaduna State Internal Revenue Service (KADIRS) has approved the creation of three new Area Revenue Offices (AROs) in the Kaduna metropolis to improve tax administration and compliance. This move a...

item.title: Tension Between Tuchel and Bellingham Shapes England's World Cup Strategy
item.description: The article explores the dynamics between England manager Thomas Tuchel and star player Jude Bellingham following their public disagreement after the Norway World Cup match. Tuchel criticized the team...

item.title: Imogen Mashazi's Private Flights Linked to Corruption Scandal at Ekurhuleni Municipality
item.description: Imogen Mashazi, a former city manager implicated in corruption at Ekurhuleni Municipality, has been linked to extravagant private flights funded by contractors tied to the looting scandal. Documents a...

item.title: Successful rescue of Oriire pupils praised by Alli and security agencies
item.description: Sharafadeen Alli, APC governorship candidate in Oyo State, commended President Tinubu and security agencies for rescuing abducted Oriire pupils and teachers. The operation, which saw the arrest of eig...

item.title: Abuja housing crisis: low-income workers struggle with unaffordable rents and limited options
item.description: Abuja, Nigeria’s capital, faces a severe housing crisis as rapid urbanization and population growth outpace affordable housing supply. Despite national policies and private investments, over 1.7 milli...

item.title: Bathurst stables target Dubbo racing meeting with promising horses
item.description: Two prominent stables from Bathurst, Angus Stewart and Gayna Williams, are preparing a strategic move at the Dubbo racing meeting, focusing on their standout three-year-olds. De Joker, a rising star u...

item.title: Valery Fabrikant, Concordia University shooter, dies in prison after long incarceration
item.description: Valery Fabrikant, the man who killed four colleagues at Concordia University in 2006, died in prison in 2024 after serving a 25-year sentence. The article details his turbulent life, including his his...

item.title: Lacson Proposes On-Site Witnesses to Expedite Sara Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: Senator-judge Panfilo Lacson suggested that witnesses scheduled to testify each week should be present at the Senate premises to avoid delays in the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte. H...

item.title: Former Tory Minister Ann Widdecombe's Murder Investigation: 28-Year-Old Arrested
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a former Tory minister and prominent political figure, was found dead at her home in Haytor, South Yorkshire, on 9 July 2026. The incident has sparked a murder investigation, with auth...

item.title: Andrea Johnson faces scrutiny over alleged information leak
item.description: The article discusses advocate Andrea Johnson, head of the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption, who is under investigation for allegedly leaking sensitive information to suspect Maj-Gen Feroz...

item.title: Argentina advances to World Cup semi-finals after dramatic 3-1 victory over Switzerland
item.description: Argentina secured a hard-fought 3-1 victory over Switzerland in a World Cup quarter-final clash, advancing to the semi-finals. The match, held at Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, saw Julián Alvarez s...

item.title: Christine McVie's Early Recognition at the 1969 Melody Maker Awards
item.description: This article recounts the career milestone of Christine Perfect, who later became Christine McVie of Fleetwood Mac. In 1969, while performing with her band Chicken Shack, she was voted 'Top Female Sin...

item.title: NMC Reinforces 2016 Arbitral Ruling as Collective Filipino Heritage
item.description: The National Maritime Council (NMC) emphasized that the 2016 arbitral ruling on the West Philippine Sea is a shared national asset, not tied to any political entity. Marking the ruling's 10th annivers...
